rulings.Case Title: PRINCESS ABROGAR VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Defendants Supplemental Special Interrogatories And Requests For Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Her Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2025 Line 1. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Defendants Supplemental Special Interrogatories And Requests For Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Her Counsel. Defendants' Motion to Compel Plaintiff's Responses to Defendants' Supplemental Special Interrogatories and Requests For Documents is granted in part. Plaintiff failed to timely respond to Defendant's supplemental special interrogatories and supplemental request for production. While Plaintiff responded to the supplemental form interrogatories, she did not respond to the supplemental special interrogatories and supplemental request for production on December 4, 2024. (Le Decl., par. 6.) Moreover, Plaintiff admits that she did not provide verifications until December 11, 2024. (Nelson Decl., par. 9.) Verifications are necessary to make the responses code compliant. (CCP 2030.210(a) [respond to interrogatories under oath]; 2031.250 [response to production demand under oath].) On January 2, 2025, Plaintiff served the supplemental responses. This further shows that Plaintiff did not timely and properly respond to the discovery requests. The court concludes that: (1) the January 2, 2025, responses are substantially code compliant so further production is unnecessary; and, (2) Plaintiff waived all objections except for the attorney-client privilege and work product doctrine per Defendants' concession. The court finds Plaintiff's actions with respect to the discovery requests at issue were not substantially justified. Plaintiff and her counsel, jointly and severally, shall pay to Defendant $4,320 as sanctions. The sanctions shall be payable by February 3, 2025. Under CRC 3.1348, sanctions are appropriate where responses are served after the motion was filed. Defendants' notice of motion provided sufficient notice that sanctions were being sought against counsel and counsel's need to show blamelessness. (Blumenthal v. Superior Court (1980) 103 Cal.App.3d 317, 319-321.) For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES ZORDANI ET AL VS. GIGSTER INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2025, Line 3, CROSS-DEFENDANT ELASTECH SERVICES LLC'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS. Cross-defendant Elastech Services, LLC's motion to quash is GRANTED. The plaintiff (or cross-complainant) bears the initial burden of showing minimum contacts to establish jurisdiction. (Integral Development Corp. v. Weissenbach (2002) 99 Cal.App.4th 576, 584 ["Even though a motion to quash service of summons is defendant's motion, the initial burden of proof is on the plaintiff to show the minimum contacts justifying the imposition of personal jurisdiction."].) In its August 26, 2024 order, the court found cross-complainant Gigster, LLC ("Gigster") failed to meet this burden and granted it leave to conduct jurisdictional discovery. Gigster has not submitted any supplemental filings. Thus, the record is as it was in August 2024. The court, therefore, affirms its finding Gigster failed to meet its jurisdictional burden and grants the motion to quash service of the cross-complaint and summons on Cross-defendant Elastech Services, LLC. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. GIGSMART, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Unopposed Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Stipulated Final Judgment And Permanent Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 16, 2025, Line 6, PLAINTIFF THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA BY AND THROUGH DAVID CHIU, CITY ATTORNEY FOR THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Entry Of Stipulated Final Judgment And Permanent Injunction. Plaintiff The People of California's Motion For Entry Of Stipulated Final Judgment And Permanent Injunction, which is unopposed, is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the court will execute the order portion of the [Proposed] Final Judgment and Injunction, filed as Exhibit C to the Eliasoph Declaration and file the executed stipulation and order. The People are ordered to submit a proposed order identical to this tentative consistent with Local Rule 8.3(G).
